LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: PLAINTIFF DID NOT GIVE NOTICE THAT SHE WILL APPEAR TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT GAVE PLAINTIFF A CHANCE TO STATE HER OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL SHARPE AND UNITED FOOD AND COMMERCIAL WORKERS LOCAL 648'S MOTION OF DEFENDANTS MICHAEL SHARPE AND LOCAL 648 TO DECLARE PLAINTIFF A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT; PROVIDE SECURITY; DISMISS ACTION; AND PROHIBIT PLAINTIFF FROM FILING NEW LAWSUITS WITHOUT COURT PERMISSION IS GRANTED. ON JANUARY 31, 2014 AND FEBRUARY 27, 2014, THE COURT ORDERED PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS. THE COURT WARNED PLAINTIFF THAT THE FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH ITS ORDER MAY RESULT IN THE MOTION BEING GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE THE COURTESY COPIES. THEREFORE, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.
